_doc: Chapter 1, Round 1 — Phase 1 fact-finding. Cula examines Halina on key facts supporting the procedural-breach (defective service) argument. Witness_cooperation resource; fact-flags gate Phase 2 citations.

phase_1._doc: Fact-finding block. Cula has 3-4 turns to press Halina on: (a) how long she lived there, (b) whether landlord communicated address change, (c) where she actually received notice (if at all). Each press option sets a fact-flag.
phase_1.opening_prompt: Dr. A. Cula, you have the opportunity to examine the witness on matters of fact.

press_options.display_text: How long have you lived in that apartment?
press_options.halina_response: Twenty-three years. Since 1962. My family has always lived there.
press_options.follow_up: The tenancy is continuous. No gap. No break.
press_options.flavor: Halina's voice steadies. This is the core of it.

press_options.display_text: Did the landlord ever tell you the street number was changing?
press_options.halina_response: Never. Not a word. One day the postman said the old address was no good. I found out by accident.
press_options.follow_up: No official notice. No letter from the landlord. Discovery, not disclosure.

press_options.display_text: Where did the eviction notice go? Did you ever see it?
press_options.halina_response: To number seven on the old street. Not my house. I never saw it. The postman tried to find number seven-after-renumbering, gave up, and left it with someone else.
press_options.follow_up: Notice went to a place that was not her home. She had no way to know it existed.
press_options.flavor: The procedural breach narrows to a point.

press_options.display_text: What warning did you have that you might be evicted?
press_options.halina_response: None. The first I knew was the bailiff at the door, three weeks after the hearing where I could not appear.
press_options.follow_up: She had no notice of a hearing. She could not attend a proceeding she did not know existed.

witness_cooperation_thresholds._doc: If witness_cooperation remaining >= 4, player unlocks all fact-flags and enters Phase 2 with maximum citations available. 2–3: weak facts, limited citations. Below 2: minimal facts, Phase 2 is an uphill climb.

phase_2._doc: Closing argument block (skeleton). Judge counter-questions gate by Phase 1 fact-flags. Available citations keyed to established facts.
phase_2.judge_opening: Counsel, you have the opportunity for closing remarks.

counter_questions.judge_text: The file records service to the old address. The form was satisfied.
counter_questions.effectiveness_notes: Player must cite a judgment with service_of_process + procedural_fairness tags to be super-effective. service_of_process alone is only effective.

counter_questions.judge_text: The landlord had no obligation to advise the tenant of municipal address changes.
counter_questions.effectiveness_notes: Counter-argument leans on margin_of_appreciation. Player must establish that landlord's silence + service failure together violate fair-hearing rights.

victory_branches._doc: Full victory unlocks the procedural reprieve. Weak victory gives the reprieve but the case outcome is marked as weaker for Ch2 reputation flags.

completion_flags.chapter1_round1_facts_established: depends on witness_cooperation at Phase 1 end
completion_flags._doc: Fact-flags survive into chapter1.json quest state and gate Phase 2 citations at runtime.
